entry.title: Career Guidance: Is SAP MM a good career choice for an absolute fresher?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am a recent graduate/fresher and am currently exploring different career paths. I am seriously thinking about starting a career in SAP MM (Materials Management), but I have absolutely no prior training or experience in it yet. Since I haven't started training yet, I’d love to connect to get your honest advice on how the job market and opportunities look for absolute beginners right now

Before I invest my time and money into learning this module, I would love to get some honest advice from working professionals:

1. How do the job opportunities look for absolute freshers starting from scratch today?
2. Is it possible to get an entry-level job without an IT or supply chain background?
3. Should I look for an authorized training institute, or are free resources (like SAP Learning Hub) enough to crack a junior role?
4. What are the common entry-level roles I should target (e.g., End-User vs. Junior Consultant)?

Any guidance on the learning path or market reality would be highly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: CFDI Idoc Solution: Idoc not linked with billing document
entry.content:
Dear support-team,

usually outgouing Idocs are linked with the triggering document in SAP - for example:

Billing document triggers INVOICE02 Idoc. Then Idoc is linked with billing document. You can see the link (or relationship)

  • in VF02/VF03 under Services for Object --> Relationsships
  • WE02/WE05/BD87 under Services for Object --> Relationsships

For our mexican company code we have customized the CFDI Idoc solution which also creates Idoc triggered by billing document. In this solution a different Idoc Typs is beeing used (MXCFDIREQ002). As we noticed this Idoc is not linked with the billing document as it is done for INVOIC02 Idocs.

Please provide information on how the Idocs for CFDI Idoc solution can be linked to the billing document.

Thanks in advance and best regards.
Alex Dürl

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 19 - CDS source formatter
entry.content:
There is great support for CAP development in IDEs such as VS Code and IntelliJ. Moreover, because the editor features for CDS are made available in the form of a language server (using the Language Server Protocol or LSP), they can also be used in other IDEs and editors, such as Neovim.

The CDS language server can be installed globally with:

npm i -g @sap/cds-lsp

Why would you do this? Well, for one thing, this gives us a very useful command line tool that can be used to format CDS source.

What is that command line tool called (the name of the command itself)?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: Tax Amount Not Visible in Standard Purchase Order Workflow Approval Screen
entry.content:
In the standard SAP Purchase Order workflow approval process, the approval screen currently displays only the Net Order Value and Tax Code details. However, the business users have requested that the Tax Amount also be displayed within the same approval window.

At present, users are reluctant to navigate to the Manage Purchase Orders application to review the tax details separately and expect all relevant financial information to be available directly during the approval process.

We have reviewed the available SAP documentation and community discussions and understand that displaying the Tax Amount in the standard workflow approval screen is not supported by standard SAP functionality.

Kindly review this requirement and advise on the best-fit solution or any possible enhancement approach to address this business need.

entry.title: Check and then Re-Check Your Cloud Foundry Org and Spaces for Correct User Assignments
entry.content:
Summary: A user can have zero access to SAP BTP Global and SubAccount and still be able to create, read, edit, or delete every service key in BTP! All you need is access at the Cloud Foundry level. Your landscape can be compromised leading to data privacy issues and business continuity can be disrupted significantly. Read more to find out how.

Most of the organizations use SAP BTP Integration Suite to connect systems across their landscape. A standard pattern is exposing HTTP endpoints (or other inbound endpoints) to third party systems so they can trigger integration flows.

To authenticate against those endpoints, you create service keys on the Process Integration Runtime service instance (integration-flow). These are basically API credentials in a way which we call Service keys (the other API credentials are available in the API Management - Developer Hub) and they live at the Cloud Foundry space tied to the service instance. Anyone with those credentials can call your integration endpoints directly and send or read data.

 

BTP admins generally manage user access carefully at SubAccount and Global Account level but generally find it too taxing to manage carefully at Cloud Foundry org and space level. When offboarding someone, most admins remove the user from the SubAccount and Global Account either manually or using IAS and then stop there. If that person still has any kind of CF access, they retain full access to every service key -- and they don't need the BTP cockpit to use it. In fact they won't have access to these using the BTP Cockpit. All they need is CF CLI or CF API to access these. The CF CLI and CF API are available to anyone on the internet for download, and a few commands is all it takes to create, read or delete any service key in the BTP environment.

I have found this security hole at many customers I am working with. The team has removed the user from the SubAccount but the user is still active in Cloud Foundry with no one having bothered to remove them from there thinking that removing them from SubAccount using IAS is enough. It is not! Even if you remove someone through IAS you must separately ensure they are fully removed from CF membership, not just their roles.

Concrete Example: say Target orders flow into S/4HANA via Integration Suite secured by service keys. A disgruntled ex-employee with no BTP cockpit access but still active in CF can delete every one of those keys. Integrations go down, orders stop getting received, and the business stops!

SAP does offer a connector to automate CF user and role management via SAP IPS (Identity Provisioning Service): https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/streamlining-user-management-integrating-sap-integration-suite-with-sap/ba-p/14069705

If you have used, please comment and share you experience especially on how easy it is to use it.

A Big thanks to the SAP BTP xID tool because of which I was able to find this issue.

In the meanwhile I urge all SAP BTP Administrators to check their CF Orgs and Spaces for any assigned users who should not be there.

entry.title: Why Thank You Awards Deserve More Attention in SAP SuccessFactors Reward and Recognition
entry.content:
One of the most under implemented features in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation is a Reward and Recognition ‘Thank You’ program.  When organisations consider implementing reward and recognition programs, the conversation often turns to budget or the affordability of monetary awards.  But a recognition program does not always need to be monetary to be meaningful to the employees it acknowledges.  A SuccessFactors Thank You Reward and Recognition program provides a platform for a non-monetary acknowledgement of appreciation.  These programs give organisations a way to recognise employee contributions, encourage positive behaviours, and strengthen a culture of appreciation without adding a direct cash award cost.

Peer to peer recognition or manager to employee awards are both supported recognition configuration options.   This allows for organisational recognition to happen in the flow of daily work and provide acknowledgement of employee contributions in the moment.  Of particular importance, is the ability for the employee’s manager to be included in the award notification to understand additional contributions occurring outside of their line of sight.

Where Thank You Awards can add value

  • Recognising employees who support work outside their formal role
  • Acknowledging collaboration that occurs outside a manager’s line of sight
  • Tying acknowledgement and outcomes back to organisation values and goals
  • Encouragement of feedback during the daily flow of work
  • Extending recognition where monetary rewards are not possible

Thank you award programs are part of the standard Reward and Recognition module and can be implemented with relatively light touch partner support.  Awards can be configured based on specific categories or levels, recognition certificates can be generated, and email notifications can be sent to both recipients and their direct managers.

If your organisation has SAP SuccessFactors Compensation or Variable Pay module licenses, a Thank You program can be a great way to make recognition more holistic and complete.  They offer a low-cost mechanism to acknowledge employee contributions and support a culture of ongoing frequent appreciation.

Award Nomination

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Award Certificate

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Best Practices for Reward and Recognition – Thank You Awards

entry.title: 使用API创建交货单库存不足提醒
entry.content:
业务需求

客户在使用API创建外向交货单时，当库存不足小于销售订单数量时，API会直接生成外向交货单并取库存数量，这样会导致客户外围系统与S/4数据不一致，并且会无效占据库存。

系统实现

案例销售订单，行项目产品ZTG12数量100

库存数量剩下90

方法1

使用BADI：LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE (Modify Standard and Custom Fields for Delivery Header and Items) 逻辑当库存数量小于销售订单行项目数量时，自定义字段赋值提醒：该产品库存不足

1.创建自定义字段YY1_Message

2.创建自定义逻辑并且发布

IF DOCUMENTPROCESSINGMODE = 'CREATE'.
CONSTANTS:
  lc_plant  TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
  lc_vkorg  TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
  lc_vtweg  TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
  lc_spart  TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

DATA:
  lv_so_qty TYPE menge_d,
  lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

move-corresponding delivery_document_in to delivery_document_out.
delivery_document_items_out = delivery_document_items_in.
LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item).

  SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
    FROM i_salesorderitem
    WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
      AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
    INTO @LV_so_qty.
  " 读取物料的库存数量
  SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
    FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
    WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
    INTO @LV_stock.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    CONTINUE. " 未找到对应销售订单行项目，跳过
  ENDIF.

  " 比较库存数量与销售订单数量
  IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
    " 更新自定义字段
    DELIVERY_DOCUMENT_OUT-yy1_message_dlh = 'Warning: 该物料库存数量不足。'.
  ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

3.创建外向交货单，系统会自动读取库存数量

保存后，查看交货单抬头自定义字段：

方法2

使用BADI：LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK (Last Checks Before Saving the Delivery)

逻辑当库存数量小于销售订单行项目数量时，系统报Error，这里不能设置报Warning，因为API创建时，会直接跳过Warning无视BADI。

 CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant    TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg    TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg    TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart    TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

    DATA:
    lv_so_qty    TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

    IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
      LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item) .
      IF ls_item-plant = lc_plant.
      " 读取销售订单行项目的订单数量
      SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
      " 读取物料的库存数量
      SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
      FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
      INTO @LV_stock.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CONTINUE. " No SO item found – skip this delivery item
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
        message-messagetype = 'W'. " saving of the delivery document will be warned
        message-messagetext = |Warning: 该物料库存数量不足|.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

 

创建交货单

感谢观看

entry.title: Re: Intercompany STO Return Process for Damaged Goods wit...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the standard way to handle intercompany STO returns after billing is through a Return Purchase Order (Return STO) process, not a customer return. This ensures proper logistics reversal and automatic financial settlement, including credit/debit memo creation and clearing against the original invoices.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 19 - CDS source formatter
Re: CN72 is not capturing the ECP Data for PS Projects.
entry.content:
Your proposed solution of extracting CKIS entries and storing them in a Z-table with date/timestamp is the right way forward. This will allow:

  • Historical snapshots for comparison reporting.
  • Flexibility to design retention and archiving logic.
  • Integration with Fiori apps for reporting.


Also, to handle ~18M records efficiently:

  • Partition by period
  • Index key fields
  • Archive beyond retention
  • Consider delta storage if feasible

entry.title: Re: Free Goods YA00 Access Sequence in S/4HANA Cloud Publ...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the free goods condition type YA00 uses a fixed, predefined access sequence that cannot be changed. Customers cannot create new access sequences or extend the standard ones through SSCUI, CBC, or expert configuration.

The SAP‑recommended alternatives are:

  • Use custom condition types in pricing if additional criteria are needed.
  • Apply in‑app extensibility (BAdIs) to influence free goods determination logic.
  • For complex rules, consider side‑by‑side extensions on SAP BTP.

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement Integration (1EG)
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Bank Statement Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

To integrate bank statements from Kyriba SFTP server to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can use the scope item 1EG. This scope item allows for both manual and automated processing of bank statements.

Key Points:

  • Scope Item 1EG: It supports the integration of bank statements, including formats like MT940, through the communication scenario SAP_COM_0316.
  • API Bank Statement - Post:  This API requires mapping of the bank statement files (e.g., MT940) to the API service. Direct upload of these files without mapping is not possible. For more informations, see 3190518 - Bank statements not processed successfully when using APIs .

Steps for Integration:

  1. Mapping Requirement: You need to map the bank statement files to the API service. This involves defining a format mapping that aligns with the API requirements. For more information, see Test Your Custom Bank Statement Definitions in Map Format Data .
  2. Automated Processing: Once the mapping is set up, the integration can be automated, eliminating manual intervention.

For more details, see the following information:

Bank Statement - Post (API)

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Tax Amount Not Visible in Standard Purchase Order Wor...
entry.content: Since the standard workflow task cannot be modified to show tax amount, the SAP‑recommended path is extensibility: either add a custom field via in‑app extensibility (if available in your release) or design a side‑by‑side extension on BTP to enrich the approval screen. This ensures compliance with cloud restrictions while meeting the business need.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 19 - CDS source formatter
Re: Add dynamic Current Period Logic (Current year Januar...
entry.content:
Hi ,
Assuming you have Calmonth time dimension in my case, I had 0calmonth

var currentDate = new Date();
var currentYear = currentDate.getFullYear().toString();

// 1. Get the current month number (1 to 12) - crntly getting eg. June, change this as required
var currentMonthNum = currentDate.getMonth() + 1;

// 2. Add a leading zero and take the last 2 digits
var currentMonthStr = ("0" + currentMonthNum.toString()).slice(-2);

// 3. Combine into YYYYMM format
var currentYearMonth = currentYear + currentMonthStr;

Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("0CALMONTH", currentYearMonth);

entry.title: Re: Output Parameter Determination: extend the condition...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Extending Condition Parameters for Handling Unit Labels

To print material-specific Handling Unit (HU) labels using the material/product ID, you can extend the condition parameters to include standard fields like ‘Material’. Here are the steps you can follow:

  1. Create a Custom Field:

    •  Use the ‘Custom Fields and Logic’ app to create a new custom field using the business context ‘Handling Unit’. For more information, see How to Extend your Custom Form Template with Custom Fields.
    • Enter a label and choose a field type, such as text.
    • Enable the usage for Output Parameter Determination for Handling Units.
    • Save and publish your custom field.

  2. Refresh Condition Parameters:

    • After publishing the custom field, go to the ‘Output Parameter Determination’ app.
    • Select ‘Handling Unit’ from the ‘Show Rules for’ dropdown list.
    • Right-click on the dropdown list and choose ‘Refresh Condition Parameters of Application’. How to Add Custom Fields as Condition Parameters in Output Parameter Determination
    • Confirm the dialog box to update the structure.

  3. Add Custom Field to Condition Column:

    • Switch to edit mode and open the table settings dialog.
    • Choose ‘Insert Column’ and select ‘From Context Data Objects’.
    • Add the custom field you created.
    • Maintain your conditions in the newly added column.
    • Save and activate the table.

By following these steps, you can extend the condition parameters to include the material field, allowing you to print material-specific HU labels.

 

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Spaces & Pages – Automatically hide empty pages for u...
entry.content:
Hello,

we can confirm that the ability to hide empty Spaces and Pages in SAP Fiori Launchpad was not available in 2023. This functionality was introduced in a later release, and is addressed by SAP Note 3635191 (Hide empty Spaces & Pages in SAP Fiori Launchpad), which became available in 2025.

The behavior you are experiencing in your current version is therefore working as designed — it is a known functional limitation of that release. To leverage this feature, an upgrade or the application of the relevant SAP Note would be required.

Please let us know if you have any further questions or if you would like guidance on the upgrade path.

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP Cloud Identity Services and Microsoft Entra ID for Joule
entry.content:
@satyach_1411 MS Entra sso handshake can´t be done in iframe. So you need an authentication before it comes to Joule iframe. I suggest setting the same conditional auth. for your work zone (or even for the whole btp subaccount oidc parent application) and other apps where you integrate joule. So when you access the platform, sso with corporate IdP is done and Joule can use that cookie / session without new authentication.

For Joule it´s needed to align SNI and conditional auth. settings with your platforms.

entry.title: Re: Intermediary Bank Setup for Customer Payments in SAP...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Payment Routing Through Intermediary Banks in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

To address the requirement of routing payments through an intermediary bank in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can use the Manage Bank Chains application. This approach allows you to specify intermediary banks for payment transactions.

Steps to Set Up Intermediary Banks

  1. Access the Manage Bank Chains App

    • Use the app to create and edit bank chains for your company.
    • Bank chains are used to make payments that are transferred through up to three intermediary banks before reaching the beneficiary bank.

  2. Set Up Bank Chains

    • Determine a combination of intermediary banks that you have previously defined.
    • Specify the order of intermediary banks based on factors such as house bank, supplier’s bank details, customer’s bank details, currency, and payment method supplement.

  3. Prerequisites

    • Ensure that the payment method used requires a bank chain. Bank chains are only determined for payments that need them.
    • Bank chains are transferred each time a payment medium is created: 2863538 - Issue with bank chains

Mandatory Details for Intermediary Banks

  • SWIFT/BIC Code: It is essential to have the SWIFT/BIC code for the intermediary bank.
  • Account Number and IBAN: While the SWIFT/BIC code is crucial, having an account number and IBAN can be beneficial for complete bank chain setup, although not mandatory.

Alternative Solutions

If the Manage Bank Chains application  does not meet your needs, consider using SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity for more advanced bank communication scenarios. This service provides direct bank communication and can automate the routing of payment messages. For more information, see Cash and Liquidity Management - Manage Bank Chains.

Best Practices

  • Regularly review and update your bank chain configurations to ensure they align with your business requirements.
  • Test the payment routing process thoroughly to confirm that payments are correctly routed through the intermediary banks.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud Sales - CDS View to retrieve Mode of...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

To connect your Sales Order or linked Delivery Document to the mode of transport in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can utilize CDS views that provide transportation-related data. Here are some CDS views that might be relevant for your requirement:

CDS Views for Transportation Management

  1. Transportation Mode

    • CDS View Name: I_TransportationMode_2
    • Purpose: This view retrieves information about transportation modes, including the mode of transport codes.
    • Important Fields: TransportationMode, TransportationModeCategory

    • Transportation Mode Category 

      • CDS View Name: I_TransportationModeCategory
      • Purpose: Provides supported values for transportation mode categories, such as Road, Rail, Sea, and Air.
      • Important Fields: TransportationModeCategory

      • Transportation Order Document Reference 

        • CDS View Name: I_TranspOrdDocRef_2
        • Purpose: Retrieves document references relevant for the whole transportation order.
        • Important Fields: TransportationOrderDocRefUUID, TranspOrdDocReferenceID

          These CDS views can help you retrieve the mode of transport code, which you can then use to connect the Dangerous Goods Text with your order. You can access these views using the View Browser app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

           

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: SAP CALM transport sequencing/reordering of release Transport
entry.content:
We need to re‑import 1000+ transports into a new UAT system (A32) for our upcoming UAT cycle. In the previous cycle (Q32), these transports were imported manually through STMS in a custom sequence that differed from the original release order due to functional dependencies.

All transports were originally released to the target group /QAS/, which includes both A32 and Q32. As soon as the transports were released from the DEV system (S32), they appeared in the import buffers of both A32 and Q32.

For the new UAT cycle in A32, we are now using SAP Cloud ALM Transport Management instead of STMS. However, CALM is enforcing the original release order of the transports and does not allow us to modify or reorder the sequence within the Feature or Deployment views.

Our requirement is to import these transports in a specific, revised sequence, matching the order used during the previous UAT cycle. We need confirmation on whether CALM supports custom import sequencing for already‑released transports, and if so, the steps required to enable or configure this capability.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP CALM transport sequencing/reordering of release T...
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

The import sequence cannot be changed or altered if the import is triggered by SAP Cloud ALM Features or the Features app. 

If you really feel that you need to change the import sequence of Transport Requests, the only option is:

  1. Unassign the relevant Transport Requests from any SAP Cloud ALM Features that they are assigned to. 
  2. Manually remove all the relevant Transport Requests from the import queue of the target system:client.
  3. Manually add the removed Transport Requests to the import queue of the target system:client again, this time in the desired import sequence. 
  4. Assign the Transport Requests to SAP Cloud ALM Features. 

Please note, it is not sufficient to simply unassign a Transport Request from a Feature with lots of Transport Requests, and then assign it to a Feature where the Transport Request will be alone. In the short term, you will be able to control the import sequence by performing several individual Feature deployments (e.g. triggering several "tp IMPORT" commands in the right sequence).

But this could cause problems downstream. If a mass deployment is later performed by selecting multiple Features and using the option to schedule their deployment in SAP Cloud ALM, this will trigger the "tp IMPORT SUBSET" command. So the actual import buffer sequence will be used to import the Transport Requests, thus breaking your manual import sequence efforts. To avoid this, please ensure that the above procedure is followed in full so that the real import sequence is adjusted before any involvement by SAP Cloud ALM Features.

Sincerely,

SAP Support

entry.title: Cross Midnight-overtime hours that start at midnight during working day preceding an holiday
entry.content:
Hi,

We have an issue in time valuations in which we can't understand the behavior of the system. Any help would be appreciated.

So, Three records are created:
1.    If we create a record of overtime for on working day starting 00:00 – 08:00, 
2.    If we create a record of overtime for a non working day (Saturday) starting 00:00 – 08:00

For these two situations trace retrieves the same information properly in time valuations in rules, when Valuation Type is “Create Time Record”, and verifies if the clock time record Start of Time Record and End of Time Record is Greater than or Equal To (>=) 10:00 PM/10:01 PM.

Start of Time Record

Start Type

Fixed Clock Time Value  

Start Time

10:00 PM  

End of Time Record

End Type

Fixed Clock Time Value  

End Time

10:01 PM  

 

  1. However, if we create a record for a non working day (Holiday) starting 00:00 – 08:00, trace retrieves different information in time valuations, then incorrect compensation for overtime during the early hours of holidays.

System automatically shifts midnight starts (00:00) to the next wokring day due to cross-midnight configurations. This causes time valuation to process the hours under a regular working day rule instead of a holiday/weekend rule.

 

When a time record is created starting at midnight, system adds +1 day indicator due to WS Day Model configuration. Due to Cross-Midnight configuration (which must be configured that way), this record is assigned to the next logical day, which is a working day because it has planned working time therefore  time valuation results retrieve different results incorrectly. 

System should read the day that refers to overtime start time, regardless overtime start time of that day is 23h59 or 24:00 or 24:01.

This way, when it comes to paying overtime wage type, since time valuation doesn't detect that the workday starts on a holiday (non working day) it assumes the next logical day, which is a working day, overtime is not paid correctly.

How can we overcome this issue, once the system cannot recognize that 00:00 on Day X belongs to overtime pay eligibility of Day X, regardless of the "+1 day" Work Schedule Day Model indicator. How can we overcome this issue, so that system treats overtime starting at 00:00 as the exact start of the day for valuation purposes, aligning clock-time recording with the actual status of that day.

entry.title: F-92 : error AAPO190 avoid asset sales
entry.content:
We need to sales an asset to a customer but transaction F-92 is not allowing to manage this due to warning message AAPO190.

Asset NBV is equal to 25K ARS and is going to be sold for 30K ARS.

How F-92 is recorded :

- First item on customer BP with posting Key 01 and amount 30K ARS

- Then offsetting item on Clearing Account for Revenue from Asset Sale with posting key 50

> Here assign cost center (same as asset to be sold) and flag tickbox 'Asset retirement'; then enter asset number to be sold and tick option 'Complete retirement'

> At that time system is raising warning message AAPO190 and avoid to post document (tickbox 'Asset retirmeent' is reset);.

> Then as per KBS 3094563; we did change asset screen layout to maintain Functional Area in related asset masterdata but field remains greyout in transaction AS02.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: Announcements & What It Means for the SAP Partner Ecosystem
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire 2026:

Announcements & What It Means for the SAP Partner Ecosystem

A comprehensive look at the most AI-dense SAP event in history — and its implications for SIs, ISVs, and technology alliances.

50+

Joule Assistants

200+

Specialized Agents

€100M

Partner AI Fund

35%+

Migration Effort Reduction

11

Strategic Partnerships

Note: This blog is intended for those who watched SAP Sapphire 2026 and want to understand its implications — particularly partners — and for those who could not attend and want a quick summary. The views expressed are personal.

SAP Sapphire 2026 in Orlando was the most AI-dense keynote in SAP history. CEO Christian Klein didn't unveil a roadmap — he unveiled capabilities shipping now. The Autonomous Enterprise isn't a future-state vision; it's a live repositioning of SAP from software that records what people do to software that does the work itself.

For SIs, ISVs, and technology alliances, this is a directional shift — the most significant reset for the partner ecosystem since HANA. The traditional model centred on configuration and training is making way for a new one centred on AI strategy, agent governance, innovation, and continuous value delivery.

 

  1.   The Autonomous Enterprise — The Vision & the Investment

"Eighty percent accuracy isn't sufficient when running mission-critical businesses. AI must work at enterprise grade — accurate, compliant, and secure."  — Christian Klein, CEO, SAP

SAP is all-in on AI. The architecture rests on three pillars:

 

Three Pillars of the Autonomous Enterprise

SAP Business AI Platform  —  A unified build-and-govern layer: BTP + Business Data Cloud + AI Foundation, with the SAP Knowledge Graph at its core

SAP Autonomous Suite  —  200+ specialized agents & 50+ Joule Assistants across Finance, Spend, Supply Chain, HCM, and CX — shipping now

Joule Work  —  The new human interface replacing traditional app navigation. Natural language in, business execution out.

To back up the vision, SAP committed €100 million to partners who deploy AI assistants or build new agents on Joule Studio. The H&M live demo on stage — Store Intelligence Agents powering an AI in-store concierge — showed the vision already in production.

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  One-time ERP projects make way to ongoing AI engagements — retainers, outcome SLAs, and agent fleet management

→  New high-value services: AI governance, decision auditing, agent lifecycle management

→  Deep domain expertise in Finance, HR, and Supply Chain now matters more than configuration skills

→  SAP is treating partners as co-creators of the Autonomous Enterprise — not just implementers

 

  1.   SAP Business AI Platform — One Foundation for Everything

SAP unified BTP, Business Data Cloud, and SAP Business AI into a single platform. At its core: the SAP Knowledge Graph encoding 452,000 tables and 7.3 million data fields — fifty years of ERP engineering made machine-readable and AI-ready.

 

Platform Components

 

Component

Detail

SAP Knowledge Graph

452,000 tables · 7.3M data fields · 50 years of ERP logic encoded semantically

Anthropic Claude (Primary Model)

Named primary reasoning model for Joule — multi-step enterprise-grade reasoning with full compliance controls

Joule Studio 2.0

GA Q3 2026 — AI-first dev env. integrating n8n (workflow), Vercel (design), NVIDIA OpenShell (secure runtime)

SAP AI Agent Hub

Q3 2026 — Central marketplace for discovering, managing & governing agents. 680+ partner submissions already

Prior Labs / SAP-RPT-1.5

€1B+ investment — tops TabArena benchmark; 3.5× better than LLMs on structured enterprise predictions

Dremio (Open Lakehouse)

Q3 2026 acquisition — extends BDC with open-format analytics at scale

Microsoft Fabric Connect

GA Now — SAP data flows natively into Fabric; zero-copy, no duplication

AWS Zero-Copy (Athena)

GA Now — SAP data accessible via Amazon Athena with no data movement required

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  Joule Studio is the default build environment — partners who get production agents deployed early win the pipeline

→  BDC + Dremio opens analytics engagements well beyond ERP boundaries

→  Tabular Foundation Models (Prior Labs) create a new prediction layer — churn, cash flow, risk — where partners can build recurring revenue services

 

  1.   Joule Work — The Biggest UX Shift Since Fiori

Joule Work abandons traditional app navigation entirely. Users describe what they need in natural language; Joule coordinates data, workflows, and agents to execute. It is already live on mobile, with web in early access and desktop coming in Q2 2026.

 

Channel

Status

Mobile App

Available NOW — full Joule Work experience on mobile

Web Client

Early Adopter Care — GA targeted H2 2026

Desktop App

Planned Q2 2026 — bridges SAP with local files, email & documents

Voice (LiveKit)

Early Adopter Care — real-time voice AI for field, factory & warehouse

 

Interoperability — Open by Design

Protocol

Detail

MCP (Model Context Protocol)

Available Now — Joule connects natively to Microsoft Copilot and third-party agents

A2A (Agent-to-Agent)

GA Q4 2026 — bidirectional integration with Salesforce Agentforce and ServiceNow

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  This is the biggest interface shift since Fiori — significant change management and training opportunity

→  Focus shifts from teaching screen navigation to designing intent libraries, assistant behaviours by role, and governance playbooks

→  Voice-first Joule opens entirely new use cases in manufacturing, warehousing, and field operations

 

  1.   The Autonomous Suite — AI Across Every Business Function

SAP deployed Joule Assistants domain by domain — 48 assistants in total across five major functions. This isn't a future roadmap; most reach GA between now and Q3 2026.

 

Domain

Assistants  ·  Key Change

Finance

7 Assistants  ·  Continuous close, real-time planning, billing, regulatory compliance

Spend Management

11 Assistants  ·  Full source-to-pay automation — Ariba, Fieldglass, expenses

HCM

13 Assistants (ALL GA June 2026)  ·  Real-time workforce decisions, AI-driven learning

Customer Experience

11 Assistants  ·  End-to-end customer journeys with Google Cloud, Parloa, Adyen

Supply Chain

6 Assistants + 60+ Agents  ·  Predictive ops, factory-edge AI (NVIDIA), RWE case

 

Finance — 7 Joule Assistants (GA Q3 2026)

Finance follows a Sense–Reason–Act model: detect signals, evaluate constraints in real time, connect plans to execution — automatically. The headline flagship is SAP Enterprise Planning, which moves finance from periodic cycles to continuous steering.

 

  • Financial Closing Assistant: Compresses close cycle from weeks to days
  • Financial Planning Assistant: Continuous planning integrated with SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Billing Assistant: Detects inconsistencies before invoice generation
  • Governance Assistant: CSRD and regulatory compliance tracking
  • Tax & Compliance Assistant: BEPS Pillar 2 and global e-invoicing mandates
  • Accounts Receivable Assistant: Churn prediction and automated dunning
  • Cash & Treasury Assistant: Real-time liquidity visibility and allocation

 

Spend Management — 11 Joule Assistants

The full source-to-pay lifecycle is covered: Category Management, Sourcing, Supplier Management, Contracts, Requisition, Buying, Receiving, Invoicing, Services Procurement, Travel, and Expense Management. SAP Ariba Buying is GA now with agentic AI-led orchestration.

 

HCM — 13 Joule Assistants (ALL GA June 2026)

The largest single-function rollout. All 13 assistants reach GA in June 2026, spanning the complete employee lifecycle: Payroll, Recruiting (SmartRecruiters integration), Onboarding, HR Service, Core HR, Time, Upskilling, Talent Acquisition, Talent Development, Talent Retention, Workforce Planning, Organizational Modeling, and HR Analytics.

 

Customer Experience — 11 Joule Assistants (Most GA Q3 2026)

From content and campaigns through to order management, sales, and service — 11 assistants cover the full CX chain. Notable partnerships: Google Cloud (Gemini + Universal Commerce Protocol), Parloa (voice AI in SAP Service Cloud), and embedded payments via Adyen, Checkout.com, and PayPal.

 

Supply Chain — 6 Assistants + 60+ Agents

Six Joule Assistants (Product Design, Manufacturing, Asset & Service, Planning/IBP, Logistics, Business Network) plus 60+ specialized agents. NVIDIA powers factory-edge AI for sub-second quality inspection. RWE demonstrated autonomous wind turbine maintenance live on stage.

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  Every domain now creates two revenue streams: initial agent deployment and ongoing optimisation

→  The engagement model shifts from project delivery to continuous outcomes — measured by days-to-close, fill rate, time-to-hire

→  Packaged offerings like 'Autonomous Close in 90 Days' or 'AI-Powered Hire-to-Retire' become viable GTM plays

→  62% of C-suite executives report dissatisfaction with how people data connects to business performance — HCM is a major opening

 

  1.   Migration & Modernisation — The Largest Near-Term Revenue Opportunity

 

35%+

ERP Migration Effort Reduction

3

Joule Assistants in RISE Year 1

3

S/4HANA Release Cycles Certified

 

Agent-led transformation tooling automates system analysis, code remediation, configuration, and testing — cutting overall migration effort by more than a third. Key changes:

 

  • RISE with SAP — Restructured: Now activates 3 Joule Assistants contractually in Year 1. ECC customers can access select AI scenarios immediately before full migration completes
  • SAP GROW: Full Joule portfolio access from day one for mid-market cloud-first customers
  • Clean Core Certification: Formally certifies BTP extensions as upgrade-compatible across three S/4HANA Cloud release cycles — creates a repeatable assessment service every ECC customer needs
  • Palantir AIP + Accenture: GA Q3 2026 — SAP Endorsed App status; Accenture as global strategic services partner for complex data migrations

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  Clean Core Assessments are a repeatable, billable service — thousands of ECC customers need certification, remediation, and migration roadmaps

→  The delivery model is shifting: fewer senior architects, more junior resources guided by AI tooling — margins expand, more deals become viable

→  ECC customers can now start receiving AI value before migration is complete — use this to accelerate stalled deals

 

  1.   11 Strategic Partnerships Reshaping the Ecosystem

SAP announced 11 major alliances spanning AI reasoning, cloud infrastructure, data, customer experience, and interoperability. For partners, the strategic imperative is to choose depth over breadth.

 

Partner

Strategic Significance for You

Anthropic

Claude is the primary reasoning model powering Joule — learn to design multi-step agent workflows

NVIDIA

Factory-edge AI runtime (OpenShell in Joule Studio) + OT/IT convergence for industrial clients

Microsoft

Build dual SAP + Azure practices; joint GTM in Azure-heavy accounts; Copilot A2A (Q4 2026)

Google Cloud

CX and commerce opportunities; Joule A2A + Gemini Enterprise + Merchant Center integration

AWS

Unified SAP + non-SAP analytics via zero-copy BDC Connect — no data movement required

Palantir

Complex data migration tooling; evaluate AIP certification (GA Q3 2026)

Accenture

Global strategic services partner for Palantir AIP and complex S/4 migrations

n8n

Visual workflow builder in Joule Studio — enables citizen developers in agent creation

Vercel

Design tooling in Joule Studio — accelerates storefront development for CX practices

LiveKit

Real-time voice AI for Joule Work — voice-first field, factory & logistics scenarios

Parloa

AI agents in SAP Service Cloud — end-to-end voice and digital service resolution

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  Don't try to do everything — pick 1–2 hyperscaler partnerships and go deep with joint GTM motions

→  Anthropic + Claude is now the AI reasoning standard inside SAP — invest in Joule agent design competency

→  LiveKit and n8n open citizen-developer and voice-first markets that traditional SAP partners haven't competed in

 

  1.   Partner Ecosystem — €100M Business AI Fund

"A Symphony of Partnership to Ring in the Era of the Autonomous Enterprise."  — Karl Fahrbach, Chief Partner Officer, SAP

The €100M Business AI Partner-Led Adoption Program is structured into four service packages, each with escalating requirements and incentives:

 

Package

What It Requires

Adoption

Entry level: deploy existing AI assistants for customers with structured enablement & support

Launch

Build and deploy at least one custom agent via Joule Studio with partner-led GTM

Performance

Demonstrate measurable business outcomes from deployed agents with escalating AI requirements

Enterprise

Comprehensive AI strategy delivery + full Joule Studio mastery + global partner designation

 

Additional Partner Programs

Validated Partner Designation — New certification for comprehensive SAP Business AI & data strategy expertise

Partner Agent Race to SAP TechEd — 680+ agent submissions received; new round focused on production-deployed Joule Studio agents

Partner Enablement Curriculum — Covering sales, presales, consulting & dev roles via regional workshops, webinars & Hack2Build sprints

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  Apply for the €100M fund now — scope an industry-specific agent (ISVs) or deployment accelerator (SIs) as your entry point

→  The 'Launch' tier is the fastest on-ramp: build one Joule Studio agent and you are in the program

→  680+ agent submissions already received — first movers who build now establish market position before the field crowds

 

  1.   Industry AI — Domain Logic Built In Across 26 Sectors

SAP is embedding sector-specific AI scenarios directly into the Autonomous Suite. Seven industries are live at launch, with 26 sectors at GA:

 

Industry

What's Live

Utilities

Autonomous asset management — RWE offshore wind turbine maintenance (live on stage)

Manufacturing

Factory-edge AI with NVIDIA: quality inspection, anomaly detection, production excellence agents

Life Sciences

Compliance-aware manufacturing & regulatory documentation for pharma and medical devices

Consumer & Retail

Unified commerce, revenue growth management, AI in-store concierge — H&M live keynote demo

Oil, Gas & Chemicals

Asset-intensive scenarios via Fieldglass: contractor lifecycle, credential verification, time tracking

Engineering & Construction

Project and resource management agents with supply chain integration

+ 20 More Industries

Agriculture, Banking, Insurance, and 17 others — full coverage at GA

 

💡  What This Means for SAP Partners

→  Every one of these 26 industries represents an addressable market for a vertical-specific Joule agent

→  Partners with deep domain expertise in utilities, pharma, or manufacturing have a moat — SAP needs their knowledge to make these agents work

→  Fieldglass-led Oil & Gas and Utilities plays are particularly underserved — significant greenfield opportunity

 

  1.   What's Shipping and When

This is not a roadmap — the capabilities below are confirmed delivery milestones:

 

Timeline

What's Available

Now (May 2026)

Business AI Platform · Joule Work Mobile · Ariba Buying AI · Fieldglass SOW AI · Microsoft Fabric Connect · AWS Zero-Copy

Q2 2026

Finance Assistants (Close, Billing, Tax, AR) · All 13 HCM Assistants · CX Sales/Shopping Assistants · Ariba Invoicing AI

Q3 2026

SAP Enterprise Planning · Revenue/Tax AI · CX Campaign Assistant · Palantir AIP Extension · Dremio acquisition close · Joule Studio 2.0 · SAP AI Agent Hub

Q4 2026

Joule Work Desktop + A2A Protocols · Governance & Sustainability Assistants · Salesforce Agentforce + ServiceNow interoperability

Nov 2026

AI Workforce Planning · Organizational Modeling Assistant · Upskilling & Reskilling Assistants

 

  1.   Your Partner Playbook — Four Actions to Take Now

The window to build first-mover advantage is open. Based on the announcements and their strategic implications, here is what SAP partners should do immediately:

 

Action 1: Get Agent-Ready Before Your Clients Ask

Map the 50+ Joule Assistants to your existing practice areas

Build a deployment methodology for your top 5 most relevant assistants

Register for Early Adopter Care programmes — the early insight is invaluable

Start building Joule Studio competency now, not after GA

 

Action 2: Apply for the €100M Partner Fund

Identify your entry point — Launch tier is the fastest (one deployed Joule Studio agent)

ISVs: scope an industry-specific agent aligned to your vertical

SIs: build a deployment accelerator or clean core assessment service

The fund is finite — first movers receive the most support

 

Action 3: Mine the Installed ECC Base

Launch Clean Core Assessments for your existing customers now

Thousands of ECC customers need certification, remediation, and a migration roadmap

Use the RISE restructuring (AI in Year 1) to reopen stalled migration conversations

35%+ effort reduction with agent-led tooling makes previously unviable deals profitable

 

Action 4: Sell Outcomes, Not Uptime

Retire 'keep the lights on' as a primary service offering

Replace it with 'optimise the agent fleet' — measured by days-to-close, fill rate, time-to-hire

Build packaged offerings: 'Autonomous Close in 90 Days', 'AI Hire-to-Retire', 'Supply Chain Sense & Respond'

Design SLAs around business KPIs, not system availability

 

The Bottom Line

"SAP Sapphire 2026 ends the ERP era and begins the Autonomous Enterprise era — the most significant reset for the partner ecosystem since HANA."

SAP came to Orlando not with slides but with receipts. H&M and RWE weren't demos — they were production deployments shown on a global stage. The 50+ Joule Assistants, 200+ agents, €100M fund, and Anthropic Claude as the reasoning backbone represent a live, shipping platform shift.

 

The partners who thrive in this new era will move beyond technical SAP skills into AI strategy, agent governance, and domain advisory. The goal is to become a trusted advisor on how organisations operate autonomously — not just how they implement software.

The €100M fund, Early Adopter Care programmes, and Joule Studio GA lower the barriers to entry. The question is not whether to act — it's how fast.

entry.title: The Future of ABAP Development: SAP Joule, AI Assistants, and Agentic Development Capabilities
entry.content:
The Future of ABAP Development: SAP Joule, AI Assistants, and Agentic Development Capabilities

How SAP's Joule AI, community-driven YAAI tools, and the emerging agentic AI paradigm are transforming ABAP development from traditional coding to intelligent, goal-driven automation — a synthesis of SAP's official capabilities and open-source innovations.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

ABAP development is undergoing its most significant transformation since the language's inception. SAP's Joule for Developers brings purpose-built ABAP LLMs trained on 250+ million lines of code, while community projects like YAAI provide open-source alternatives for AI integration. The emerging "agentic" paradigm — where AI doesn't just suggest code but plans, executes, and validates entire development workflows — represents the next frontier. This blog synthesizes insights from SAP's official agentic ABAP capabilities and community-driven YAAI tools to provide a comprehensive view of where ABAP development is headed in 2025-2026 and beyond.

01  Introduction: The AI Revolution in ABAP

For decades, ABAP development has followed a predictable pattern: developers write code, test it, debug it, and deploy it. The tools have evolved — from SE38 to ADT to VS Code — but the fundamental workflow remained human-centric. That era is ending.

Two parallel forces are converging to transform ABAP development:

 

  • SAP's Official AI Stack — Joule for Developers with ABAP-trained LLMs, integrated into ADT and VS Code, providing code generation, explanation, and increasingly agentic capabilities.
  • Community Innovation — Open-source projects like YAAI (Yet Another ABAP AI) that enable developers to integrate any LLM into ABAP systems with function calling, RAG, and agent-building capabilities.

Together, these represent not just new tools, but a fundamental shift in how ABAP code will be written, maintained, and evolved. This blog explores both paths and what they mean for ABAP developers preparing for 2026 and beyond.

 

02  SAP Joule for Developers: The Official AI Copilot

What is Joule for Developers?

SAP Joule for Developers is SAP's AI-powered development assistant, specifically designed for SAP technologies. Unlike generic AI coding assistants, Joule includes purpose-built capabilities for ABAP:

 

  • ABAP-Trained LLM — Trained on over 250 million lines of ABAP code and 30 million lines of CDS, plus extensive SAP documentation.
  • Deep Integration — Native integration with ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse and VS Code extensions.
  • Context Awareness — Understands your current project, open files, and SAP-specific patterns.

 

Core Capabilities (Available Now)

Code Generation & Completion

Joule provides intelligent code suggestions as you type, understanding ABAP syntax, patterns, and best practices:

  • Predictive ghost text completion based on context and comments
  • Full code snippet generation from natural language descriptions
  • RAP artifact generation (entities, behaviors, service bindings)
  • ABAP Cloud-compliant code suggestions aligned with clean-core principles

 

Code Explanation & Learning

For developers working with unfamiliar code or learning new patterns:

  • Plain-language explanations of ABAP code, CDS views, classes, and interfaces
  • Contextual Q&A about ABAP development concepts
  • Links to relevant SAP documentation and examples

 

Unit Test Generation

One of the most valuable features for quality assurance:

  • Automatic generation of ABAP Unit tests for classes and methods
  • CDS test class generation for data model validation
  • Test case suggestions based on code analysis

 

KEY TAKEAWAY

Joule is not a generic coding AI with ABAP added as an afterthought. It's purpose-built for SAP development, trained on SAP's own codebase, and designed to enforce SAP best practices like clean-core and RAP patterns.

 

Availability Across SAP Platforms

Joule for Developers ABAP AI capabilities are available across:

 

Platform

Availability

Key Features

SAP BTP ABAP Environment

Generally Available

Full Joule integration, GenAI Hub access

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Generally Available

Native integration, extensibility assistant

S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

2021+ versions

Requires Joule Base subscription

SAP ECC / On-Premise

Limited

Via BTP side-by-side extension only

 

 

03  YAAI: Community-Driven ABAP AI Tools

What is YAAI?

YAAI (Yet Another ABAP AI) is an open-source project that brings AI capabilities directly into ABAP systems. Unlike Joule, which is SAP's managed service, YAAI gives developers complete control over their AI integration.

 

The project consists of two main components: yaai (the backend library) and yaai_ui (the frontend components).

 

Core Features of YAAI

Multi-Provider LLM Support

Unlike vendor-locked solutions, YAAI supports multiple LLM providers:

  • OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o, etc.)
  • Ollama (for self-hosted, private LLMs)
  • Google Gemini
  • Any provider with OpenAI-compatible APIs (Nvidia, Azure OpenAI, etc.)

 

Conversation Management

YAAI maintains conversation state directly in your ABAP application:

  • Multi-turn conversation history
  • Session persistence across interactions
  • Context management for complex workflows

 

Function Calling (Tool Use)

This is where YAAI becomes powerful for building agents:

  • Expose ABAP methods as callable "tools" to the LLM
  • LLM can decide when to call your business logic
  • Foundation for agentic behavior patterns

 

YAAI FUNCTION CALLING

* Example: Exposing an ABAP method as a tool

DATA(lo_chat) = zcl_yaai_chat=>create( ).

 

* Register a tool the LLM can call

lo_chat->register_tool(

  iv_name        = 'get_customer_orders'

  iv_description = 'Retrieves orders for a customer ID'

  io_handler     = me  " Implements the tool interface

).

 

* LLM can now decide to call this tool when relevant

 

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

YAAI includes RAG capabilities for grounding AI responses in your enterprise data:

  • Connect LLM responses to SAP business data
  • Provide context-aware answers from your systems
  • Reduce hallucination with factual grounding

 

YAAI Cloud: Built for Agent Development

The cloud version of YAAI (yaai_cloud) was specifically designed for building AI agents on SAP BTP ABAP Environment:

 

💡 INSIGHT

"The Cloud version was built with AI agent development as its primary focus, and all the tooling is designed to support ABAP developers in building and testing AI agents." — YAAI Cloud documentation

 

Key additions in YAAI Cloud:

  • Pre-built library of ABAP development tools (based on XCO library)
  • Agent testing and debugging infrastructure
  • Integration patterns for BTP ABAP Environment

 

YAAI vs Joule: When to Use What

 

Aspect

SAP Joule

YAAI

Control

SAP-managed service

Full developer control

LLM Provider

SAP's ABAP LLM only

Any provider (OpenAI, Ollama, etc.)

Integration

Native ADT/VS Code

Pure ABAP libraries

Use Case

Developer productivity

Custom AI solutions & agents

Cost Model

Joule subscription

Open source + LLM API costs

Best For

Standard ABAP development

Custom business AI applications

 

 

04  The Agentic Revolution: Beyond Code Completion

What is Agentic AI?

Agentic AI represents a fundamental shift from "AI as assistant" to "AI as autonomous collaborator." Instead of just suggesting code, agentic AI can:

 

  • Understand Goals — Interpret high-level objectives ("migrate this custom code to RAP")
  • Plan Actions — Break down goals into concrete steps
  • Execute Tasks — Actually perform the development work
  • Validate Results — Test, verify, and iterate on outcomes
  • Collaborate — Work with other agents and human developers

 

SAP's Agentic ABAP Architecture

SAP is building an agentic infrastructure specifically for ABAP development:

 

ABAP MCP Server (Model Context Protocol)

MCP is an open standard that allows AI agents to interact with external tools and systems. SAP's ABAP MCP server:

  • Exposes ABAP development capabilities to any MCP-compatible agent
  • Enables existing ABAP AI use cases as tools for agents
  • Provides a "universal language" for agent-to-ABAP communication

 

Agent Orchestration Layer

SAP is wrapping individual ABAP AI skills into coordinated agentic flows:

 

AGENTIC WORKFLOW

Agentic Flow Example: "Create a RAP service for customer orders"

 

Agent Actions:

1. UNDERSTAND → Analyze existing table structure and requirements

2. GENERATE   → Create CDS views, behavior definitions, service binding

3. IMPLEMENT  → Add validations, determinations, business logic

4. EXPOSE     → Create OData service and Fiori app shell

5. TEST       → Generate and run unit tests

6. VALIDATE   → Check clean-core compliance, fix issues

 

Human Role: Define goal, review results, approve deployment

 

Agentic Use Cases Coming in 2025-2026

 

1. End-to-End RAP Application Creation

From a natural language description, agents will create complete RAP applications:

  • Entity definitions and CDS views
  • Behavior definitions with validations and determinations
  • Service bindings and OData exposure
  • Fiori Elements app scaffolding
  • Unit tests and documentation

 

2. Custom Code Migration

One of SAP's primary focus areas for agentic ABAP is S/4HANA migration:

  • Analyze legacy custom code for migration requirements
  • Suggest modernization paths (RAP, released APIs, clean core)
  • Transform code automatically where possible
  • Generate tests to validate migrated functionality

 

3. Extensibility Assistant

For S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, agentic assistants help with:

  • Finding appropriate extension points (BADIs, events)
  • Adding custom fields with proper business context
  • Implementing value helps and validations
  • Navigating the clean-core extension model

 

🚀 FUTURE OUTLOOK

SAP's roadmap indicates that by 2026, ABAP Agentic AI flows will become standard, with existing use cases migrating into agent-based patterns. Agents will be available even for older SAP Cloud ERP Private versions (2021+), democratizing access to AI-driven development.

 

 

05  Community Innovations: abapgit-agent and Beyond

The abapgit-agent Pattern

One innovative community project bridges external AI coding assistants (like Claude Code or GitHub Copilot) with ABAP systems through Git:

 

ABAPGIT-AGENT ARCHITECTURE

External AI Agent Workflow:

 

┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐

│   AI Agent      │────▶│    Git      │────▶│   ABAP      │

│ (Claude/Copilot)│     │ Repository  │     │   System    │

└─────────────────┘     └─────────────┘     └─────────────┘

        │                                          │

        │◀─────────── Feedback Loop ───────────────│

 

Commands available to the AI:

• pull    - Pull from Git and activate objects

• inspect - Run syntax check / Code Inspector

• unit    - Run AUnit tests

• view    - Explore tables, classes, CDS views

 

This creates an agentic loop where external AI can iteratively improve ABAP code based on feedback from the actual SAP system — syntax errors, Code Inspector findings, and test results flow back to the AI for correction.

 

The Ecosystem is Expanding

The community is actively exploring agentic patterns beyond SAP's official offerings:

  • Custom agents for specific business processes
  • Integration with VS Code and other modern IDEs
  • Hybrid approaches combining Joule and custom agents
  • Private/on-premise AI deployment via Ollama and similar

 

 

06  Roadmap: 2025 Sets the Pace, 2026 Wins the Race

2025 Milestones

  • ABAP AI skills become mainstream (code assist, tests, explanations)
  • Joule for Developers widely adopted for unit test and code generation
  • Q4 2025: ABAP AI LLMs available on GenAI Hub
  • Fine-tuned ABAP LLMs (ABAP 1) on AI Foundation

 

2026 Direction

  • Q2 2026: Full Agents for ABAP, S/4HANA AI deployment, VS Code extension
  • ABAP Agentic AI flows become the standard development pattern
  • Existing use cases migrate into agent-based architecture
  • ~40 Joule Agents and 2,100+ pre-delivered skills across SAP platform
  • MCP becomes the standard integration protocol for ABAP AI

 

⚠️ IMPORTANT

Licensing Note: For S/4HANA Cloud Public, Joule for Developers ABAP AI requires a Joule Base subscription. The limited standalone "Joule for Developers ABAP AI" license expired in September 2025 — newer consumption is via standard Joule offerings.

 

 

07  Practical Guidance: Preparing for the Future

For ABAP Developers

 

  1. Enable Joule in your IDE — Whether ADT or VS Code, activate ABAP AI capabilities now. Start with code explanation and unit test generation.
  2. Learn RAP and Clean Core — AI tools are optimized for modern ABAP patterns. Understanding RAP, CDS, and released APIs will maximize AI assistance.
  3. Experiment with YAAI — Even if Joule is your primary tool, understanding how to build custom AI integrations opens new possibilities.
  4. Think in workflows, not tasks — Agentic AI rewards end-to-end thinking. Practice describing goals rather than steps.

 

For Technical Architects

 

  • Evaluate your AI strategy: SAP-managed (Joule) vs. custom (YAAI) vs. hybrid
  • Plan for MCP integration as the standard agent protocol
  • Identify migration candidates that could benefit from agentic assistance
  • Establish governance for AI-generated code (review, testing, compliance)

 

For Organizations

 

  • Budget for Joule subscriptions if using S/4HANA Cloud
  • Upskill ABAP teams on AI collaboration patterns
  • Create sandbox environments for AI experimentation
  • Define policies for AI code contributions and intellectual property

 

 

08  Conclusion: The ABAP Developer's New Reality

The future of ABAP development is not about AI replacing developers — it's about AI amplifying what developers can achieve. The combination of SAP's purpose-built Joule capabilities and community innovations like YAAI creates an ecosystem where:

 

  • Routine tasks (boilerplate, tests, documentation) become automated
  • Complex tasks (migration, modernization, optimization) become assisted
  • Creative tasks (architecture, design, business logic) remain human-driven but AI-informed

 

The developers who thrive in this new era will be those who learn to collaborate with AI effectively — providing clear goals, validating outputs, and focusing their expertise where it matters most.

 

Whether you choose SAP's official Joule path, the community's YAAI approach, or a hybrid of both, the time to start is now. The agentic revolution in ABAP isn't coming — it's already here.

entry.title: Restore True Time-Based Scaling in Compensation History Graph within Latest People Profile
entry.content:
In SAP SuccessFactors Employee Profile, the Compensation History graph available in the Latest People Profile displays salary change events with equal spacing between data points, regardless of the actual time elapsed between the corresponding effective dates.

As a result, salary changes that occurred several years apart are displayed with the same visual distance as changes that occurred only a few months apart.

Business Challenge

The current visualization does not accurately represent the timeline of compensation evolution. Users reviewing an employee's salary history cannot immediately understand the duration between compensation changes, as the graph treats the timeline as a sequence of events rather than a date-based scale.

This behavior differs from the previous profile experience, where data points were positioned proportionally according to their effective dates, providing a more accurate and intuitive representation of salary progression over time.

For example:

  • A salary change between January 2019 and December 2020 should appear significantly farther apart than a change between December 2020 and March 2021.
  • In the current Latest People Profile, both intervals are displayed with identical spacing.

Requested Enhancement

Introduce an option, or restore the previous standard behavior, so that compensation history graphs in the Latest People Profile use a true date-based timeline.

Data points should be positioned proportionally according to their effective dates, ensuring that visual distances accurately reflect the actual time elapsed between compensation events.

Business Value

  • Improved readability and interpretation of compensation history.
  • More accurate visualization of employee salary progression.
  • Better support for HR business partners, managers, and compensation specialists during salary reviews.
  • Consistency with the behavior previously available in the legacy profile experience.
  • Enhanced user experience and data transparency.

Expected Benefit

Users will be able to immediately understand both the magnitude and timing of salary changes without needing to manually analyze individual effective dates. This will improve decision-making during compensation reviews, talent discussions, and employee history analysis.

With kind regards

Kristian

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Alternative to Document Type ER for Intercompany Travel Expense Recha
entry.content: Dear Marcel, not sure if I fully understood your challenge. So basically we would recommend for Travel Expenses to use SAP Concur. Within SAP Concur employees can assign travel costs to cost objects in other company codes and with 4AN we ensure a posting to employee cost center and a subsequent Mgmt Accounting (CO) reposting to the specified cost object. This cross company posting will then in addition be picked up by 4AN ICO billing. Described also here: Intercompany Cost Allocations in S/4HANA Cloud https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/intercompany-cost-allocations-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13442721 in chapter 'Concur intercompany posting'. The process for integration 3rd party travel expense postings is also described there. It is possible to use other doc types than ER, but the pre-requisites as well as posting process (via WBS incl. Pers. number) remain. So if you just need another doc type there will be a solution.

entry.title: UI5 Plugins for Coding Agents: Now Supporting GitHub Copilot
entry.content:
UI5 Plugins for Coding Agents: Now Supporting GitHub Copilot

When we first launched this project, we called it "UI5 Plugins for Claude" — because Claude Code was the coding agent we built it for. That name made sense at the time. But the world of AI-assisted development has moved quickly, and so has this project.

Today we're announcing a new name, GitHub Copilot support, and three new skills that extend what the plugin knows about UI5 development.

A Name That Reflects Reality

The project is now called UI5 Plugins for Coding Agents.

The rename isn't cosmetic. From the beginning, the goal was never to lock UI5 developers into a single AI tool — it was to bring deep, framework-aware assistance to whatever coding agent they use. The plugin concept (skills, MCP server, guidelines) is fundamentally agent-agnostic. The old name undersold that.

The new name says what the project actually is: a collection of plugins that make any capable coding agent a much better UI5 development partner.

GitHub Copilot Support

With v0.1.3, the UI5 plugins are now available for GitHub Copilot via Awesome GitHub Copilot.

That means UI5 developers using Copilot can now get the same assistance that Claude Code users have had — project scaffolding, API documentation access, UI5 linting, and the full set of skills described below.

Three New Skills

The ui5 plugin covers general coding standards through ui5-best-practices — async module loading, OData binding, TypeScript event handlers, CAP integration, and so on. Three recent additions extend this into areas that benefit most from dedicated, framework-specific guidance.

UI Integration Cards: ui5-best-practices-integration-cards

UI Integration Cards have very specific rules around data placement, binding syntax, and manifest structure. With v0.1.2ui5-best-practices-integration-cards encodes those rules so the agent can get cards right without trial and error: correct data block placement, destination configuration with {{destinations.name}} syntax, parameter binding with {parameters>/key/value}, manifest validation via the run_manifest_validation MCP tool, Configuration Editor patterns for dt/Configuration.js, and full analytical chart support across 44 chart types with required UIDs and feed configurations.

OPA5 Integration Tests: ui5-best-practices-opa5

Writing reliable OPA5 tests requires knowing the actual control tree of the running app — something a coding agent can't derive from source alone. With v0.1.4 ui5-best-practices-opa5 bridges that gap through tooling: pause-on-failure mode keeps the app live at the point of failure, and the TestRecorder (sap.ui.testrecorder.ControlTree, UI5 ≥ 1.147) lets the agent inspect the real control tree and generate accurate waitFor snippets from it. The skill also covers initial test configuration, multi-view setups, page object organization, and teardown patterns.

UI5 Tables: ui5-best-practices-tables

Tables are central to many UI5 freestyle apps, and with v0.1.5 ui5-best-practices-tables helps agents get them right from the start. It provides a practical selection guide across sap/m/Table, sap/ui/table/Table, sap/ui/table/TreeTable, sap/ui/comp/smarttable/SmartTable, and sap/ui/mdc/Table, while reinforcing essential patterns like correct items vs rows binding, proper ariaLabelledBy, and production-ready handling for performance, personalization, drag and drop, and data type alignment.

Installing

Claude Code

claude plugin install ui5@claude-plugins-official

GitHub Copilot

copilot plugin install ui5@awesome-copilot

Skills Only (Any Agent)

If your coding agent doesn't support the plugin concept, you can install the skills directly:

npx skills add UI5/plugins-coding-agents

You'll also need to set up the UI5 MCP server manually.

What's Next

Supporting more agents is the direction we're heading. If you're using a coding agent that isn't supported yet,  join the conversation in on GitHub UI5/plugins-coding-agents #38.

The project is open source under Apache 2.0. Contributions, feature requests, and feedback are welcome at github.com/UI5/plugins-coding-agents.
